parts.title: The Tryouts
parts.text: Nobody expected Sahil to sign up for the basketball tryouts. The **wicked** heat of summer training was brutal — 5 AM every morning, rain or shine. But Sahil's first **task** was simply to make the team, and she attacked it with a ferocious will that surprised everyone watching.

parts.title: The Doubters
parts.text: Her **peers** on the squad didn't welcome her at first. People said it wasn't **typical** for someone her age to compete at this level. She had to **refuse** to listen to the voices that told her to quit. Saanvi, the coach, used every **resource** she had to support Sahil — extra drills, film sessions, and encouragement. When a sprained ankle threatened the **border** of her season, Sahil rested one week and came back stronger.

parts.title: The Turning Point
parts.text: During the semifinal, a rival player let out a **squeal** of shock when Sahil faked past her with speed nobody expected. Sahil could feel the **spine** of the crowd's energy running through her. The **quality** of her performance in those final minutes was unlike anything she had shown before. The ball left her hands and sailed through the net, and the gymnasium erupted.

parts.title: Victory
parts.text: They won the championship by a single point in the **final** seconds. Sahil was lifted onto her teammates' shoulders. The old wooden bleachers showed some **decay** around the edges, but nobody noticed — every eye was on Sahil. Saanvi told the reporters, "She's the hardest-working athlete I've ever coached. Everyone doubted her, but she proved them all wrong." That night, Sahil looked at her medal and smiled. The underdog had become the champion.

definitions.meaning1: The column of bones running down the middle of the back; the backbone. Example: The doctor checked the X-ray to make sure the child's spine was straight and healthy.
definitions.meaning2: The narrow part of a book cover that shows the title when it sits on a shelf. Example: She read the titles on the spine of each book as she searched the library shelf.
definitions.studentChallenge: You should sit up straight to keep your ___ healthy and strong.

definitions.meaning1: Evil, cruel, or morally wrong. Example: The wicked queen in the fairy tale tried to poison the princess with a magic apple.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ villain in the movie laughed as he locked the heroes inside the dungeon.

definitions.meaning1: A piece of work or a job that needs to be done. Example: Feeding the classroom hamster every morning was her favorite task of the week.
definitions.studentChallenge: The first ___ on our list was to clean up the art supplies from the table.

definitions.meaning1: To look closely or carefully at something that is hard to see. Example: She peered through the foggy window to see if the school bus had arrived.
definitions.meaning2: A person who is the same age or has the same status as you. Example: He presented his science project to his peers in the classroom.
definitions.studentChallenge: The detective had to ___ closely at the tiny footprints left in the mud.

definitions.meaning1: To slowly break down or rot. Example: Fallen leaves decay on the forest floor.
definitions.studentChallenge: Sugar can cause your teeth to ___.

definitions.meaning1: To make a loud, high-pitched cry or sound. Example: The children squeal with delight every time they go down the big waterslide.
definitions.studentChallenge: The piglet let out a loud ___ when it was picked up from the pen.

definitions.meaning1: How good or bad something is. Example: The quality of the wool in this jumper is excellent, so it will last for many years.
definitions.meaning2: A characteristic or feature of a person or thing. Example: Kindness is one of his best qualities.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ of her artwork improved greatly after she took lessons over the summer.

definitions.meaning1: The edge or boundary of something. Example: The river forms the border between the two countries.
definitions.studentChallenge: Flowers grew along the ___ of the garden.

definitions.meaning1: To say no to something. Example: She refused to give up, even when things got hard.
Materials, money, or supplies that can be used to accomplish something. Example: The school invested its resources in new computers and library books for the students.
definitions.studentChallenge: Water, timber, and minerals are all natural ___ that we should use wisely.

definitions.meaning1: Normal and what you would usually expect. Example: A typical school day begins with math and ends with art class.
definitions.studentChallenge: It was a ___ rainy day in spring, with gray clouds covering the sky all afternoon.

definitions.meaning1: Last; coming at the end with nothing after it. Example: The final chapter of the book revealed who the mystery thief really was.
definitions.meaning2: A last test or game in a series (noun). Example: She studied hard for her math final at the end of the school year.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ question on the quiz was the hardest one of all.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: You should sit up straight to keep your ___ healthy and strong.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The first ___ on our list was to clean up the art supplies from the table.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The detective had to ___ closely at the tiny footprints left in the mud.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Fallen leaves ___ on the forest floor, slowly breaking down into soil.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Sugar can cause your teeth to ___ if you don't brush them.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The children ___ with delight every time they go down the big waterslide.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The piglet let out a loud ___ when it was picked up from the pen.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The ___ of the wool in this jumper is excellent, so it will last for many years.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The ___ of her artwork improved greatly after she took lessons over the summer.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The river forms the ___ between the two countries, running along the entire edge.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Flowers grew along the ___ of the garden, framing it like a colorful picture.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: She ___ to give up, even when things got hard and everyone else quit.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: He ___ to eat his vegetables, pushing them to the side of his plate.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The school invested its ___ in new computers and library books for the students.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Water, timber, and minerals are all natural ___ that we should use wisely.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: A ___ school day begins with math and ends with art class.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: It was a ___ rainy day in spring, with gray clouds covering the sky all afternoon.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The ___ chapter of the book revealed who the mystery thief really was.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The ___ question on the quiz was the hardest one of all.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage: Arjun had one _(1)_ job — a single piece of work assigned to him — before the school science fair ended. He had to _(2)_ closely through his magnifying glass at a sample of fallen bark to see exactly how it had begun to rot and break down. He could see where _(3)_ had turned the once-hard wood soft and crumbling. This was no _(4)_ project — it was unlike anything his classmates had attempted before. The fair was held near the _(5)_ of the school garden, right at the flowered edge between the path and the grass. He had limited _(6)_ — only a few tools and materials — so he had to plan carefully. The bark sample had a tough _(7)_ running through its center, a stiff column that held it together even as it rotted. A _(8)_ little beetle — mean and sharp-jawed — scurried out of the bark and onto Arjun's hand. The _(9)_ of his work was excellent, so careful and thorough that his teacher stopped to admire it. A smaller boy let out a sudden _(10)_ of excitement when he saw Arjun's display. Arjun would _(11)_ to give up no matter how tired he felt. The _(12)_ step was to present his findings to the judges, and he did so with perfect confidence.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
